So how do you know you have the right people? I got asked this question so many times as a digital agency owner, I personally experience the wrong people and the right people. See the wrong people are going to make you feel like you're giving them money and they're not doing their job. The right people are going to make you feel like you need to pay them more. That's an easy way of saying it, especially when it comes from the entrepreneurial mindset of just justifying payable. I can't tell you how many times I've literally handed somebody money or sent them a wire or however it is that I've paid the person and I just felt like shit on the inside because I know this person didn't deliver to the level that I needed them to. Those people I have fired in my business and I do not tolerate that but I had to learn inside of my body through feeling and my senses that that is what identified to me if I had the right people or the wrong people. Now since learning the hard way and now having all the right people I now qualify everybody who comes into my business through a series of tests and phases of how I interview them. I keep in mind I have a COO in my business and my process may be a little different than yours but I'm going to give you the exact steps that I take in my business that you should also follow in yours. See, I immediately have the person as soon as I say I need a position filled I don't even do it. I'll post the job, I'm not the one going out on my way to hire the person into my business. I have my project manager right up the hat which I'll give you a hat sample to download below and I also have my COO then post the job on ZIP recruiter and field all the interviews. My COO is the one who's out there in the field who's getting all the interviews and she is my little pit bull. You see her name is Kathy and Kathy she is somebody who I mean she absolutely crushes it in her ability to build a massive team. As I told you in one of the previous videos Kathy at one point looked over 57 different teams with over 400 people in them across the United States for a radio agency that she looked over on as an executive. That being said she now when bringing people into my business goes through this two step interview process which you can do yourself. The two step interview process is interview number one she asks a series of questions and I'm going to give you questions now. She says hello, nice to meet you, sit down, work on a video interview, hello thank you for joining us. She immediately tracks what time they showed up. You see if they're a few minutes early we encourage that, deploy that, if they're exactly on time we also encourage you to deploy that. If they're less than literally if they're one minute late we knock them we don't even do the interview. See right away hey what's going on this is you listen I saw you were one minute late and unfortunately we do track time and make sure that people are on time we're actually not going to be able to continue with the interview. We apologize we're going to have to move on to the next person. That might seem a little harsh and it might also be due to an internet connectivity issue and if it is an internet connectivity issue you give them a little bit of slack. If it's not no no interviews. That being said if you do get through the interview these are the questions you want to ask them. So what brings you in today? What is it that makes you want to work with us? What are your intentions? Listen to what they say. Ask them further say what are some of your personal goals that we can help you solve with working for our business? What past experience do you have that qualifies you to work for our company in this role? Do you have any short term or long term ideas or intentions to expand within our organization to a different role? Is there something that we need to know we need to help position or align you towards for a promotion long term? How much are you looking to get paid to accomplish this task force in business? What is it that's going to get you to the next level of productivity within our organization? This question is a trick question because if they're not already like listen I already do whatever it takes and I just fucking crush it you got to know that that person is not going to try as hard as they could and you're going to have to hold them accountable or you're going to have to consider that before you hide. That being said the next series of questions that we ask typically are dictated on whatever answers were asked or I'm sorry whatever answers were given by the questions that we asked in this preliminary question. Sometimes the questions that I like to ask are sounding like this and this is if I do interview myself I'll typically ask something different than what my COO would ask. So all those first questions are our standards this is what I ask versus what my COO would ask. I say so have you ever worked inside of a personality brand business before? Have you ever dealt with somebody like me? If you're in an environment where it's extremely high pressure and things are meant to get done in timelines where other businesses might say it's nearly impossible to accomplish those things do you complain or do you have the whatever it takes mindset? Do you define whatever it takes means for you? Do me a favor explain to me how you work as a team. In our business we work as a unit commonly and what happens to one individual happens to the team typically when it comes to one success equaling success for everybody else. Individually your COG within our system generates success for the rest of the COG wheels. How do you look at yourself when it comes to being a team member? Now there's a lot of questions I can't ask you that I want to. There's a lot of things that I really want to ask or I really want to see when it comes to behaviors or actions that would give me a better idea of who you actually are. There's two bad habits that you have. What would you say they are? It's an interesting question. Some people say oh I eat shitty food. Some people say oh I smoke or oh I go out and party on the weekends. I love this question more than anything. What are two bad habits that you still have right now? I want to know. Are you smoking? Are you eating shitty foods? You're going to have low energy throughout the day? Are you going on a party on the weekend so I'm not going to be able to use you on the weekend if an emergency happens and my website goes down and you're my developer? Oh what's the bad habits? Figure it out. Get to the bottom of it. The moral of the story is how to know you built the right team can also be done and phase number two of the interview. Now phase number two is my favorite because it statistically shows me what their words can. They give you an IQ and EQ and an aptitude test. Now the aptitude test the way that you want to word this because legally I don't know if you can give an actual quote unquote aptitude test to an employee to see if they have the aptitude to accomplish a job. So what you want to say is we want to see an employee match rate through a test that we're going to have you perform. See this employee match rate test is going to give us an idea if your current abilities match what we're looking for inside of our business. But in reality it's an aptitude test. Don't say that it's an aptitude test. You want to say that it's an employee assessment test that's going to identify an employee match test is another way to say it. It's going to identify if they're right fit for your organization. The EQ and the IQ test I think is $60 per like three tests but it's incredibly worth it because I can see the EQ, the IQ and the aptitude test from an individual with the military uses when they break people in. You can download those below. I don't know if I can give it to you for free or not so in the video I'm going to disclose they might be $60 but I'm not getting that in this affiliate link or anything. It's just might be $60 to do the test these times in interview. But once again, highly encouraged and highly recommended. It lets you know that you have the right people because in interview one, yes you got to ask some questions but in interview number two you technically got to see the actual statistics of the individual. Now interview number three typically comes and what I call interview number three and week number one in business. I explained to you guys in the previous course that I have a week one to week two period of time where I'm paying them only $500 to $750 a week or $500 to $750 for a big task or a list of tasks. That being said I also call that week an interview. That way I don't technically hire the person into my business. It's defined as a technical test legally and if I have any issues with the individual they're gone. Now if you hire the person into your business it's much harder to fire them than it is to hire them. Legally you come into a bunch of different issues and you bring them in as a W-2 employee. I have a whole different course on W-2 versus 1099 but you also know that you have the right people typically from them wanting to be on a 1099 status for the fact they want to be their quote unquote own boss. You see a subcontractor legally you can't technically tell what to do but we're going to go into more detail on that inside of the course on 1099 versus W-2. Well the story ladies and gentlemen is that as you know who the right people are the best way that you can identify even after they go through the IQ and EQ test even after they go through all the interviews and the test third week interview where you can technically fire them if they do wrong without any penalties. I still say the best identifier for this specific question on how to know when you have the right people is how you feel after you pay them. Do you feel satisfied and feel like you've got every bank for your dollar that you just put into it or do you feel sick on the inside that you just paid this person the amount that you did. That can be your true identifier not for how you know if you got the right people. This has been the how to know if you got the right people course inside of phase number seven the team building and execution phase. I'll see you inside of the next course.
